title: Baby Changing on Board Act
plain_summary: This bill says that Amtrak must put baby changing tables in their new train cars. Every train car needs to have at least one bathroom with a changing table for parents to use. The changing tables must also be in bathrooms that people with disabilities can use. This rule only applies to new trains that Amtrak buys after this law is passed. Amtrak also has to put up clear signs so parents can easily find the bathrooms with changing tables.
last_action_text: Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation. Ordered to be reported with an amendment in the nature of a substitute favorably.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Baby Changing on Board Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Installation of Baby Changing Tables on Amtrak Trains
sections.text: This section adds new rules to the federal transportation laws. The new rules require Amtrak trains to have baby changing tables in their bathrooms. An ADA-compliant restroom means a bathroom that follows the rules in the Americans with Disabilities Act to make it accessible for people with disabilities. A baby changing table is a raised surface or platform designed to safely hold a child weighing up to thirty pounds while lying flat so someone can change the child's diaper. This includes surfaces that pull out from a wall or fold down. A covered passenger rail train means any train that Amtrak owns and operates, but only trains that Amtrak buys after this law takes effect. It does not include trains that Amtrak runs but does not own. All covered trains must have a baby changing table in at least one bathroom in each train car. At least one of these bathrooms must be accessible to people with disabilities. The bathroom with the changing table must have clear signs showing that a baby changing table is available inside. The changing tables themselves must also have clear signs identifying them as changing tables.
